title: Let triage commit a homogeneous GROUP of small similar items as one cycle — amortize the ~7.6M-token per-cycle pipeline wrapper (measured >60% of cycle spend)
why: OPERATOR PRIORITIZATION 2026-07-21: token-optimization class bumped by directive ('prioritize and bump token optimization related tasks'). NOTE 2026-07-21: this is the DEGENERATE case of triage-unified-solution-synthesis (0.90) — mechanical grouping of homogeneous smalls when NO shared root cause exists. Prefer the synthesis item's design; this one stays as the fallback grouping path it must fail-open to. MEASURED (knowledge-base/research/token-usage-history-2026-07-20.md + triage-decision.json sampling): the fixed per-cycle wrapper (scout/fault-loc/coverage-gate/bug-repro/audit/adversarial/retro) averages ~7.6M cache-read tokens vs ~4.9M for build+tdd — over 60% of spend is pipeline, not work. Observed top_n averages ~1.5 (974:2, 977:1, 980:1, 981:2). inboxbatch.Classify already GROUPS similar items (triage.go:112) but triage rarely commits a whole group. Batching 3 homogeneous small items per cycle roughly halves per-item cost.
how_to_apply: Config-not-code where possible: (1) extend the triage persona/contract so a rendered inboxbatch GROUP of small same-class items (docs/config/test-only/single-package fixes) MAY be committed as one top_n entry with an explicit member list; (2) the R9.2 capacity clamp counts the GROUP as its floor-count so throughput accounting stays honest; (3) build-report must enumerate per-member ACs and the eval/ACS layer keeps per-member predicates (no blended green — a member failing must fail loudly, not hide in the group); (4) guard: heterogeneous or source-heavy items stay single-item (disjoint-file fleet lanes already cover the parallel case). Measure the delta via per-cycle usage artifacts.
evidence: triage-decision.json top_n sizes 974-981; per-phase usage aggregation cycles>=900; inboxbatch wiring go/internal/phases/triage/triage.go:112-116.
